name: WiFiPumpkin3
summary: Powerful framework for rogue access point attack.

documentation.readme: Powerful framework for rogue access point attack.

arguments.name: -i
arguments.description: set interface for create AP

arguments.name: --ignore-from-networkmanager
arguments.description: set interface for ignore from Network-Manager

arguments.name: -iNet
arguments.description: set interface for share internet to AP

arguments.name: -iNM
arguments.description: set interface for ignore from Network-Manager

arguments.name: -m
arguments.description: set wireless mode settings

arguments.name: --no-colors
arguments.description: disable terminal colors and effects.

arguments.name: -p
arguments.description: interactive sessions can be scripted with .pulp file

arguments.name: --password
arguments.description: Start the RESTful API with the specified password instead of pulling from wp3.db

arguments.name: --pulp
arguments.description: interactive sessions can be scripted with .pulp file

arguments.name: --remove-from-networkmanager
arguments.description: remove interface from Network-Manager

arguments.name: --rest
arguments.description: Run the Wp3 RESTful API.

arguments.name: --restport
arguments.description: Port to run the Wp3 RESTful API on. default is 1337

arguments.name: -rNM
arguments.description: remove interface from Network-Manager

arguments.name: -s
arguments.description: set session for continue attack

arguments.name: --username
arguments.description: Start the RESTful API with the specified username instead of pulling from wp3.db

arguments.name: --wireless-mode
arguments.description: set wireless mode settings

arguments.name: -x
arguments.description: interactive sessions can be string with

arguments.name: --xpulp
arguments.description: interactive sessions can be string with

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `WiFiPumpkin3` from its upstream repository, then run `WiFiPumpkin3 --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. Powerful framework for rogue access point attack.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category. Fits when the engagement needs: Powerful framework for rogue access point attack.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Powerful framework for rogue access point attack.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with jayofelony/pwnagotchi (score=12); pair in the chain planner.
